all_candidates.topic: Should NEET be reformed to prevent future paper leaks?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The NTA declared NEET UG 2026 re-examination results on July 16, 2026, following the cancellation of the original exam due to an alleged paper leak, sparking ongoing protests and calls for reform.

all_candidates.topic: Should restaurants be legally banned from auto-adding service charge?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) initiated action against 41 restaurants on July 19, 2026, for levying service charges by default.

all_candidates.topic: Are AI-driven "silent layoffs" harming India's IT workforce?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Reports from July 9 and July 18, 2026, indicate AI is driving "silent layoffs" and restructuring in India's IT sector, with estimates of 25,000-35,000 job losses in 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should the Education Minister resign over the NEET 2026 exam row?
all_candidates.news_anchor: On July 18, 2026, a group of senior academicians, economists, and social activists wrote to Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, urging him to resign over alleged mismanagement of NEET UG 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should E20 fuel be priced lower due to reported mileage loss?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas admitted on July 10, 2026, that E20 fuel may reduce mileage by 3-5%, while stating its focus is energy security.

all_candidates.topic: Should consumers have a choice between E20 and non-E20 petrol?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal on July 18, 2026, launched an online campaign seeking public feedback and urging the government to make both conventional and E20 petrol available.

all_candidates.topic: Should platforms face stricter deepfake removal timelines?
all_candidates.news_anchor: India notified new IT Rules 2026 to regulate AI and deepfakes, significantly reducing platform response timelines for removal.

all_candidates.topic: Should large merchants pay a small fee for UPI transactions?
all_candidates.news_anchor: A report on July 18, 2026, indicated the government is considering reinstating a small Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) for large retailers on UPI transactions.

all_candidates.topic: Should young professionals prioritize AI-resistant skills?
all_candidates.news_anchor: A Mercer report (June 23, 2026) indicates entry-level employees aged 22-27 face the greatest risk of AI-driven job replacement.

all_candidates.topic: Should government services have mandatory digital delivery?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Delhi Cabinet approved the Delhi (Right of Citizen to Time Bound and Ease of Delivery of Services) Bill, 2026, on July 15, 2026, aiming for end-to-end digital service delivery.

all_candidates.topic: Will mandatory low-floor city buses improve public transport accessibility?
all_candidates.news_anchor: India has mandated that from October 2026, all city buses produced for municipal transport must be low-floor, a policy framework detailed in July 2026 reports.

all_candidates.topic: Will stricter UPI KYC rules help prevent fraud effectively?
all_candidates.news_anchor: New UPI rules for 2026 include stricter KYC enforcement, with IDs linked to partially verified bank accounts potentially facing restrictions.

all_candidates.topic: Should Samsung replace defective Flip phones with global screen issues?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Kolkata District Consumer Commission on July 3 (reported July 17), held Samsung India guilty of service deficiency for the Galaxy Z Flip 3, ordering replacement due to global screen failure.

all_candidates.topic: Do new foreign funding rules unfairly restrict NGOs?
all_candidates.news_anchor: On June 22, 2026 (reported July 17), India's Ministry of Home Affairs adopted Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Rules, granting new powers to police NGOs receiving foreign funding.

all_candidates.topic: Do new UPI 2FA rules make digital payments safer for users?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Effective April 1, 2026, new RBI UPI rules mandate Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for every digital payment to combat fraud, with ongoing discussion about its impact.

all_candidates.topic: Will the India-UK CETA truly benefit Indian workers and trade?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and Double Contribution Convention (DCC) came into force on July 15, 2026, aiming to boost trade and exempt Indian workers from UK social security contributions.

all_candidates.topic: Is the ₹1.27 lakh crore Semicon Mission 2.0 a good investment?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Union Cabinet cleared ₹1.27 lakh crore for the second edition of the India Semiconductor Mission on July 15, 2026, aiming to attract significant investments.

all_candidates.topic: Is the July 2026 GST ITC locking beneficial for businesses?
all_candidates.news_anchor: From July 2026, Phase 2 of GSTR-3B hard locking is expected to cover ITC reporting, linking it to GSTR-2B data.

all_candidates.topic: Is the ₹5 crore e-invoicing threshold for GST too low for businesses?
all_candidates.news_anchor: From July 2026, e-invoicing became a hard mandate for GSTINs with aggregate annual turnover (AATO) over ₹5 crore in FY 2025-26.

all_candidates.topic: Is India's new 'One Nation, One Time' system beneficial?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi inaugurated the 'White Rabbit Technology'-based 'Indian Standard Time Distribution Demonstration Network' on July 17, 2026, to establish a high-precision, secure time standard.
